_meta.description: Complete v6 variant pool. Each of 13 scenario nodes has 3 clinical variants; same competency target across variants preserves psychometric scoring. Per-session selection is deterministic via djb2 hash of session.id+nodeId.
_meta.author: AIWIZN / ATEN Inc 2026
_meta.clinical_review_status: Phase-1 author draft. SME sign-off pending per Verification Report Section 6.

s1a.label: Anterolateral STEMI - hypotensive
s1a.env_title: Mr. David Osei, 58M - 'It is just indigestion'
s1a.env_meta: 15 minutes ago he walked in. Now on the monitor: ST elevation, chest pressure radiating to jaw, diaphoresis.
s1a.ecg_banner: ST ELEVATION - V2-V5 - STEMI PROTOCOL ACTIVE

s1a.label: Inferior STEMI with RV involvement
s1a.env_title: Mr. Walker Hayes, 64M - 'Like an elephant on my chest'
s1a.env_meta: Brought in by paramedics. Pain onset 35 minutes ago while shovelling snow. Diaphoretic, anxious, pointing at sternum.
s1a.ecg_banner: ST ELEVATION - II/III/aVF - INFERIOR STEMI - RV INVOLVEMENT LIKELY

s1a.label: Silent MI - atypical diabetic presentation
s1a.env_title: Mr. Theodore Okonkwo, 71M - 'Just a bit of indigestion'
s1a.env_meta: Type 2 diabetic, walked into triage 90 minutes after symptom onset. No chest pain. Subtle Q waves on ECG. Wife noticed clamminess in the car.
s1a.ecg_banner: ST DEPRESSION V4-V6 - NEW Q WAVES - SILENT MI SUSPECTED

s1a2.event: BP drops to 82/45 after nitroglycerin given
HR drops to 42 with new AV block during pre-cath workup
V-fib arrest in pre-cath holding bay - witnessed monitor change

s1b1.label: Family insists on second opinion before transport
s1b1.env_title: Family at bedside - daughter blocking gurney
s1b1.env_meta: Daughter (RN herself) demands second opinion before cath lab. Door-to-balloon clock running. Patient is alert, deferring to daughter.

s1b1.label: Language barrier delaying consent
s1b1.env_title: Patient speaks Mandarin only - consent unclear
s1b1.env_meta: No professional interpreter available. Patient nephew translating but visibly upset. Cath team waiting.
s1b1.ecg_banner: DOOR-TO-BALLOON - 41 MINUTES ELAPSED - INTERPRETER LINE 2 MIN ETA

s1b1.label: DNR status discovered mid-workflow
s1b1.env_title: Mrs. Patel, 78F - DNR found in chart, family disputes
s1b1.env_meta: DNR signed 2 weeks ago. Husband says she changed her mind yesterday but no documentation. Pain 8/10. Decision needed now.
s1b1.ecg_banner: DOOR-TO-BALLOON CLOCK PAUSED - GOALS-OF-CARE REVIEW IN PROGRESS

s1b2.label: SBAR handover to interventional cardiology
s1b2.env_title: Cath team on the line - 30 seconds for handover
s1b2.env_meta: Dr. Chen (intervention) waiting. Need: situation, key vitals, intervention so far, what is needed next. Nothing more, nothing less.
s1b2.ecg_banner: PHONE LINE OPEN - DR CHEN STANDING BY

s1b2.label: Night-shift handover with cognitive load
s1b2.env_title: Tired night intern - simplify or fully brief?
s1b2.env_meta: Intern is 14 hours into shift, looks overwhelmed. Three other patients pending. SBAR must be sharp without losing critical detail.

s1b2.label: Charge nurse expedite request
s1b2.env_title: Charge needs expedited summary in 15 seconds
s1b2.env_meta: Two STEMIs simultaneously. Charge nurse triaging. Yours is older, more stable - but the data needs to be tight to justify priority.

s1c.label: Patient refuses recommended cath
s1c.env_title: Mr. Osei refusing cath - 'I want to go home'
s1c.env_meta: Capacity intact. He understands the risk. Wife in tears. Cardiologist waiting outside. Your move respects his autonomy and protects him from preventable harm.

s1c.label: Religious refusal of blood products
s1c.env_title: Patient is Jehovah Witness - refuses transfusion if needed
s1c.env_meta: Active bleeding risk in cath. Patient firm in faith, capacity intact. Documentation needs to be precise to protect both patient and team.

s1c.label: Homeless patient wants to leave AMA
s1c.env_title: Mr. Reilly - unhoused, AMA, says he has 'somewhere to be'
s1c.env_meta: Capacity intact. Active STEMI. Has been in and out of ED before. Where is he going? What does AMA actually mean for someone with no fixed address?
s1c.ecg_banner: AMA REQUEST - SOCIAL WORK EVALUATION INDICATED

s2a.label: Stroke - last known well uncertain
s2a.env_title: Mrs. Chen, 67F - found down by neighbour
s2a.env_meta: Right-sided hemiplegia, expressive aphasia. Last seen well at family dinner 2.5 hours ago - or maybe earlier. Neighbour uncertain.
s2a.ecg_banner: NIHSS 12 - tPA WINDOW DEPENDS ON LKW VALIDATION

s2a.label: Wake-up stroke - imaging-guided window
s2a.env_title: Mr. Andersson, 58M - woke up with deficits
s2a.env_meta: Slept normally at 11pm. Awoke at 6:30am with arm weakness and slurred speech. Standard tPA window unclear; MR-guided window may apply.
s2a.ecg_banner: WAKE-UP STROKE - DWI/FLAIR MISMATCH IMAGING NEEDED

s2a.label: Atypical stroke - young patient, dissection
s2a.env_title: Ms. Patel, 32F - sudden vertigo and ataxia after yoga
s2a.env_meta: Posterior circulation symptoms. No traditional stroke risk factors. Recent neck manipulation. Vertebral dissection on differential.
s2a.ecg_banner: POSTERIOR STROKE SUSPICION - CTA NEEDED BEFORE tPA DECISION

s2a2.label: tPA window closing - timeline ambiguity
s2a2.env_meta: Family says LKW was 3 hours ago. Wife uncertain - could be 4 hours. Push tPA on optimistic estimate or hold for confirmation?

s2a2.label: Patient on anticoagulants
s2a2.env_meta: Recent atrial fib, on warfarin. INR returns at 2.8. Bleeding risk vs ischaemic damage trade-off needs to be sharp.
s2a2.ecg_banner: ANTICOAG STATUS REVIEW - tPA CONTRAINDICATION CHECK

s2a2.label: Pregnant patient - relative contraindication
s2a2.env_meta: Pregnancy historically considered tPA contraindication. Newer data more nuanced. Maternal-foetal medicine team incoming.
s2a2.ecg_banner: OBSTETRIC + STROKE CONSULT - SHARED DECISION NEEDED

s2b.label: Patient terrified of locked-in syndrome
s2b.env_title: Mr. Brennan crying, asking 'will I be a vegetable?'
s2b.env_meta: Conscious, partial right-sided weakness. Saw locked-in patient in news last month. Therapeutic communication, not false reassurance.

s2b.label: Expressive aphasia - patient frustrated to tears
s2b.env_title: Mrs. Davies cannot find the word - rage and tears
s2b.env_meta: Expressive aphasia post-stroke. Cognitively intact, emotionally devastated by inability to speak. Family upset, mistakenly assuming cognitive decline.

s2b.label: Caregiver in collapse
s2b.env_title: Husband shaking - 'I can not lose her too'
s2b.env_meta: Patient stable but critical. Husband lost brother to stroke last year. Carer mental-health crisis emerging. Two patients now.
s2b.ecg_banner: DUAL CARE - PATIENT STABLE, CAREGIVER NEEDS SUPPORT

s2c.label: Discharge - lives alone, 4th-floor walkup
s2c.env_title: Mr. Wilson - 'I am fine, send me home'
s2c.env_meta: Mild residual weakness. Lives alone, 4 flights of stairs, no railings. Insurance limits rehab placement. Real-world discharge complexity.

s2c.label: Family caregiver already burned out
s2c.env_title: Daughter - 'I cannot do this alone any more'
s2c.env_meta: Father moderately impaired. Daughter is sole caregiver, unemployed, two young children. Fragile system. Discharge here = readmission within month.

s2c.label: Language access on discharge instructions
s2c.env_meta: Patient and wife only speak Spanish. Discharge instructions in English. Niece interpreting (selectively). Real readmission risk if get this wrong.
s2c.ecg_banner: LANGUAGE ACCESS REVIEW - PROFESSIONAL INTERPRETER REQUIRED

s3a.label: Urosepsis from missed UTI
s3a.env_meta: Confusion increasing 6 hours. Mild fever, foul-smelling urine. Daughter dismissing as dementia. SIRS criteria emerging quietly.
s3a.ecg_banner: SIRS CRITERIA MET - SEPSIS BUNDLE INITIATION

s3a.label: Post-op sepsis - missed source
s3a.env_meta: Wound looks fine. Subtle tachycardia, mild fever. Surgeon dismissive. But the lactate just came back at 2.8.

s3a.label: Neutropenic fever in chemo patient
s3a.env_title: Mr. Reyes, day 10 post-chemo - first temp spike
s3a.env_meta: ANC 0.4. Temp 38.4. Single blood culture set. Source unclear. Time from spike to antibiotics is the survival metric here.

s3b.label: Attending dismissive of sepsis call
s3b.env_meta: Senior attending. You think bundle should start now. Authority gradient is real. So is the lactate of 3.2.

s3b.label: Intern overconfident, missing red flags
s3b.env_meta: Intern day 3 of rotation. You see the trajectory; they do not. How you escalate matters as much as that you escalate.

s3b.label: Charge nurse pushing back on bundle
s3b.env_title: Charge nurse - 'we do not have the staff'
s3b.env_meta: Bundle requires resources. Unit short-staffed. Charge nurse not wrong about staffing - and not right about delaying the bundle.

s3c.label: Rising lactate post-fluids
s3c.env_meta: Adequate fluids given. Lactate rising not falling. Source control inadequate. Trend tells the truth even when the number is hard to look at.

s3c.label: Worsening oxygenation - ARDS emerging
s3c.env_meta: Sepsis with respiratory failure emerging. Bilateral infiltrates new on chest X-ray. ARDS criteria forming. Intubation conversation needed.

s3c.label: Acute kidney injury developing
s3c.env_meta: Septic with new oliguria. Creatinine doubled in 24h. Renal replacement conversation. Vasopressor escalation. Multi-organ trajectory.
s3c.ecg_banner: ACUTE KIDNEY INJURY - STAGE 2 - NEPHROLOGY CONSULT

s3c2.label: ICU escalation despite team friction
s3c2.env_meta: Lactate rising. Colleague suggests waiting. Your move respects them and protects the patient. Both can be true.
s3c2.ecg_banner: ICU TRANSFER DECISION - DATA OVER COMFORT

s3c2.label: ICU full - alternative escalation
s3c2.env_meta: Patient needs ICU; ICU is full. Step-down accepting but with caveats. Transfer hospital 2 hours away. The decision is system-level, but the patient is yours.

s3c2.label: Family resisting escalation
s3c2.env_title: Family - 'no machines, she would not want that'
s3c2.env_meta: Daughter speaking for elderly mother. Mother lacks capacity. Goals of care unclear in chart. Bundle escalation vs comfort transition - need to clarify before deciding.
